reasoning: The ORIGINAL and CORRECTED formalizations have identical theorem statements. The only difference is `open Topology` (ORIGINAL) vs `open scoped Topology` (CORRECTED), which only affects which names/notations from the `Topology` namespace are opened. This does not affect the semantics of the theorem statement, which uses `MetricSpace`, `ConnectedSpace`, `Countable`, `univ`, and `Set` — none of which are shadowed by the `Topology` namespace. The informal statement ("a connected metric space having more than one point is uncountable") is faithfully captured: `[MetricSpace X]` gives the metric space, `[ConnectedSpace X]` gives connectedness, `∃ x y : X, x ≠ y` encodes "more than one point," and `¬ Countable (univ : Set X)` encodes uncountability (via the Set CoeSort to the subtype). The ORIGINAL is faithful.
